critic_raw:
CRITIQUE (Round 1):

1. [ISSUE-A] VENDOR_BASELINE_DEFAULT_CONFIG_ONLY: Receipt-specific enterprise parser was not used. The vendor's generic default is not optimized for this task.

2. [ISSUE-B] LANGUAGE_CONFIGURATION_MISMATCH: 30% of test set is bilingual; vendor ran English-only; startup was bilingual fine-tuned. This directly contaminates the accuracy comparison on the bilingual subset.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (Round 1):

[ISSUE-A] The default tier is the right comparator for the target market segment.
[ISSUE-B] Bilingual support is a genuine product advantage for Canadian bookkeeping.
adjudication_raw:
Round 1: Both issues remain open. Defender addresses commercial framing but not scientific validity of the gap claim.

Round 2:
Critic [ISSUE-A + ISSUE-B]: Both issues require empirical resolution — run vendor with receipt-specific parser and bilingual mode, measure the gap on full set and English-only subset.
Defender: Agree that the empirical test is the right resolution. The startup believes its model will still show a meaningful advantage but acknowledges the current comparison is not controlled.
[RESOLVED: ISSUE-A and ISSUE-B — both parties agree empirical test is needed]

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.measure: Exact-field accuracy on full 8,400-receipt set and on English-only subset
empirical_test.success_criterion: Startup model leads reconfigured vendor (receipt-specific parser, bilingual mode) by ≥ 5 pp on full set and leads on English-only subset
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Gap below 5 pp on full set with reconfigured vendor, or startup trails on English-only subset
